name: Lewis Nash
abstract: Lewis Nash (born December 30, 1958) is an American jazz drummer. According to Modern Drummer magazine, Nash has one of the longest discographies in jazz and has played on over 400 records, earning him the honor of Jazz's Most Valuable Player by the magazine in its May 2009 issue.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Katz
about_quotes.evidence: a recent gig I had at the Charlie Parker Festival I played with one of the young drummers ... his name is Lewis Nash
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Katz interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: one of the greatest drummers I’ve played with recently is Lewis Nash
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: I’d never played with Lewis Nash and John Goldsby in a trio setting... And I put them together for the date. We didn’t have a rehearsal, we just came into the studio, and man the recording is beautiful.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: I threw her in with like real guys: Kenny Barron, Ron Carter, Lewis Nash, Fathead, Hank Crawford. I said look, if I’m going to make a record with her I want to make it with my guys
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Buckingham
about_quotes.evidence: I had Eddie Gomez flying to Nashville from New York, and Lewis Nash on drums, and I put out the number charts

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Monheit
about_quotes.evidence: having Kenny Barron, Ron Carter and Lewis Nash play on my first album
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Monheit interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 20, 2010
